is randomly sampled. The observation space, action space,

manipulation tasks. The task is considered successful when

text:
As shown in Table XIII, several exploration-based baselines
exhibit degraded performance compared to the task-rewardonly (TR) baseline, suggesting that overly aggressive exploration may interfere with stable grasp acquisition. In particular,
RND-Dist encourages novelty in hand–object distance even
in free space, which guides the policy toward exploring noncontact behaviors and conflicts with the prolonged, surfaceenveloping finger contact required for stable grasping. In
contrast, CCGE consistently improves both success rate and
sample efficiency across all objects, demonstrating its ability
to encourage structured contact exploration without disrupting
